Container.Name: SimpleLogin-AIO
Container.Overview:
SimpleLogin is an open-source email alias service that lets you create forwarding addresses to protect your real inbox from spam, tracking, and data breaches.
This All-In-One template packages the full self-hosted stack into one Unraid container: - SimpleLogin web UI - background jobs - inbound email handler - Postfix - PostgreSQL - Redis
Beginner path: 1. Set App URL, Email Domain, Support Email, and Flask Secret. 2. Pick an SMTP relay mode if your ISP blocks outbound port 25. 3. Forward inbound TCP port 25 from your router to Unraid. 4. Start the container and wait for first-boot initialization to finish. 5. Add the required DNS records from the setup guide.
Advanced users can still point the container at external PostgreSQL and Redis by setting DB_URI and REDIS_URL. Otherwise, leave them blank and the internal services stay self-contained inside this single image.
Current upstream packaging is amd64/x86_64 only.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: email, alias, proxy, privacy, proton, forwarding, self-contained

Config.Name: Appdata Directory
Config.Description: Primary persistent directory for PostgreSQL, Redis, uploaded files, generated keys, and SimpleLogin state. Back this folder up.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: The port for the Web UI.

Config.Name: SMTP Inbound Port
Config.Description: Inbound SMTP port. Forward TCP port 25 from your router or firewall to the Unraid host so the internet can deliver mail to your aliases.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Public URL for the SimpleLogin dashboard. Example: https://app.example.com

Config.Name: Email Domain
Config.Description: The primary domain used to create aliases.

Config.Name: Support Email
Config.Description: The address from which transactional system emails (like password resets) are sent.

Config.Name: Flask Secret
Config.Description: Long random secret used for Flask sessions and application security. Use a unique high-entropy value.

Config.Name: SMTP Relay Mode
Config.Description: Choose how outbound mail should leave the container. Most residential ISPs block outbound port 25, so a relay provider is usually the safest choice.
Config.Option: Direct Delivery (Requires open outbound ISP Port 25)

Config.Name: Brevo Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'brevo'

Config.Name: Brevo Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'brevo'

Config.Name: ProtonMail SMTP Token
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'protonmail'

Config.Name: Gmail Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'gmail'

Config.Name: Gmail App Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'gmail'

Config.Name: Custom Relay Host
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'custom'. Format: [smtp.provider.com]:587

Config.Name: Custom Relay Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'custom'

Config.Name: Custom Relay Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'custom'

Config.Name: Advanced: External DB_URI
Config.Description: Leave blank to use the internal PostgreSQL instance. Set a full postgresql:// connection string only if you want an external database.

Config.Name: Advanced: External Redis URL
Config.Description: Leave blank to use the internal Redis instance. Set a full redis:// URL only if you want an external queue/cache.

Config.Name: Disable Registration
Config.Description: Set to 1 to block public registration (recommended).

Config.Name: IdP: Enable SimpleLogin SSO
Config.Description: Set to 1 to allow other apps to u0027Login with SimpleLoginu0027. This automatically generates the required JWT RS256 keys on boot.

Config.Name: Apple: API Secret
Config.Description: If you want to allow iOS users to sign in with Apple. Requires Apple Developer Portal setup.

Config.Name: Apple: MacApp API Secret
Config.Description: If using the native Mac app.

Config.Name: HIBP: API Key
Config.Description: Have I Been Pwned API Key. If provided, SimpleLogin will scan your aliases for data breaches.

Config.Name: HIBP: Scan Interval
Config.Description: How often (in days) to check HIBP for breaches.

Config.Name: SSO: OIDC Discovery URL
Config.Description: Enter the well-known URL for your self-hosted IdP (e.g. Tailscale tsidp, Authelia, Authentik). Example: https://auth.lan/.well-known/openid-configuration

Config.Name: SSO: OIDC Client ID
Config.Description: Client ID for your self-hosted OIDC provider.

Config.Name: SSO: OIDC Client Secret
Config.Description: Client Secret for your self-hosted OIDC provider.

Config.Name: SSO: OIDC Scopes
Config.Description: Scopes requested from the OIDC provider.

Config.Name: SSO: OIDC Name Field
Config.Description: The field in the OIDC user info response to use for the user name.

Config.Name: Anti-Spam: Enable SpamAssassin
Config.Description: Set to 1 to enable. Requires a separate, external SpamAssassin container running on your network (too heavy to bundle here).

Config.Name: Anti-Spam: SpamAssassin IP
Config.Description: The IP address of your external SpamAssassin container (e.g., 192.168.1.50).

Config.Name: Telemetry: Plausible Host
Config.Description: URL of your self-hosted Plausible Analytics instance (e.g., http://plausible:8000). Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: Telemetry: Plausible Domain
Config.Description: Domain name tracked in Plausible.

Config.Name: Telemetry: Sentry DSN
Config.Description: DSN URL for your self-hosted Sentry instance. Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: PGP: Keyring Directory
Config.Description: Persistent directory for your GnuPG keyring. Used to encrypt forwarded emails before they reach your real inbox.

Config.Name: PGP: Auto-Generate Server Key
Config.Description: Set to 1 to automatically generate a 4096-bit RSA server key for signing outgoing forwards. Safe to enable, but only useful for advanced users. Defaults to 0 (Disabled).

Config.Name: PGP: Private Key Path
Config.Description: Internal container path to your specific private PGP key (e.g., /pgp/my-private-key.asc) if you want SimpleLogin to sign outbound forwards.
